sprint: Sprint 26 -- Driving Season Open + B-047 + B-053
spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-06-from-spool-sprint-26-priorities.md (Spool Priority 1 -- US-292 DTC retrieval gaps) + offices/pm/backlog/B-047-pi-self-update-from-server-release-registry.md (D1-D9 resolved CIO 2026-05-05; 4 candidates filed) + offices/pm/backlog/B-053-engine-aware-sync-poll-cadence.md (Option 2 approved CIO 2026-05-05) + offices/pm/sprint_26_candidates.md (queued list)
backlogItem: Driving-season opening + B-043 phase: pi-wiring activates car-coupled lifecycle (every key-on = Pi cold boot) + B-047 phases.production-validation + B-053 phases.cadence-fix + Spool's DTC retrieval gap closure.

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-25 close baseline (V0.25.0 main@5e37494). Sprint 25 added ~10 new test files (US-284-291). Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Driving season opens this weekend (~5/9 per Spool 2026-05-06). 9 stories across 3 backlog items: US-292 closes Spool's DTC retrieval gaps (US-204 partially shipped Sprint 15 -- audit before adding); US-293/294/295/296/297 validate B-047 Pi self-update production path before car-wiring activates trigger every key-on; US-298/299/300 implement B-053 engine-aware sync cadence (CIO-approved Option 2). Plus US-295 NEW: B-047 safety-precondition audit -- specifically verify auto-update CANNOT fire during a drive once Pi is car-coupled.

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1A (P0) -- US-292 DTC retrieval gaps. TIER 1B (P1, parallel) -- US-293 + US-294 + US-296 + US-297 (B-047 production validation). TIER 1C (P1, deps US-292) -- US-295 safety-preconditions audit. TIER 2 (P2) -- US-298 (state machine M); US-300 (sync_log retention S, parallel). TIER 3 -- US-299 (deps US-298 wire-in).

THEME: 'Driving-season ready.' V0.25.0 restored engine telemetry (US-285); now we close the safety + observability + cadence gaps so the Pi handles its first car-coupled drive cycle cleanly. Per Spool: this is the baseline-building window before any mods touch the car.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline per Sprint 23/24/25 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming; all UPDATE paths verified on disk. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier (--check-feedback) active for sprint close. (c) BL-009 lesson: NO architectural ambiguity in story contracts. US-298 cadence values mandated upfront per CIO 2026-05-07 answer 'follow Spool's workflow' = post-drive batch review (IDLE=60s, ACTIVE=5s, DRAINING=single flush; CIO can override Sprint 27+ via config). US-300 retention horizon = 90 days per CIO 2026-05-07 (matches existing Pi data retention). US-292 NOT greenfield -- existing US-204 ship audited; story closes only the gaps Spool flagged. (d) Per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md: every fix story has a synthetic test that would FAIL pre-fix.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-06 sprint-26-priorities note + offices/pm/backlog/B-047 (D1-D9 resolved CIO 2026-05-05) + offices/pm/backlog/B-053 (Option 2 approved CIO 2026-05-05) + existing US-204 codebase audit (dtc_client.py + dtc_logger.py + dtc_log_schema.py shipped Sprint 15, 2026-04-20; v0005_us238_create_dtc_log.py server migration shipped Sprint 19) + CIO 2026-05-07 grooming answers (Q1 Spool's workflow = ACTIVE 5s; Q2 90-day retention).

POST-SPRINT-26 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO performs Pi-to-ignition wiring (~5/9 weekend per Spool note); insurance flip; car out of storage. (2) Drive 6 = first post-fix street drive (Spool's tuning-judgment review ritual; inbox handoff). (3) First real *drive* drive (15-20 min mixed city/highway) -- Spool's data-collection ritual. (4) Pre-mod baseline shelf: 3-5 clean drives across May-June for tuning baseline before any mods. (5) Marcus bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION V0.25.0 -> V0.26.0 at sprint close per SemVer convention (major=0 until V1.0.0 stable milestone). (6) Spool reads post-Drive-6 data + writes inbox note declaring engine-telemetry-regression CLOSED (carryforward from Sprint 25).

stories.title: Complete US-204 DTC retrieval -- Spool gap closure
stories.intent: Spool 2026-05-06 P1 ask. US-204 (Spool Data v2 Story 3) shipped Sprint 15 (2026-04-20) -- src/pi/obdii/dtc_client.py + dtc_logger.py + dtc_log_schema.py exist. But Spool's specific asks may not all be met by the Sprint 15 ship: (a) Mode 03 query every 30s DURING drive (not just at drive_start), (b) Mode 07 query at drive_end (current dtc_logger says 'session-start + MIL-event'), (c) Dashboard footer with DTC count + most-recent code. Pre-flight audit verifies what's already in place vs what's a gap; story closes ONLY the gaps. Per Sprint 23/24/25 closure-in-fact pattern (US-273/283): if audit shows everything's wired, ship passes:true with no code edits + closure-in-fact-pre-existed completionNotes.

stories.title: B-047 production e2e drill: real Pi + real server release registry
stories.intent: Per B-047 Sprint 26 candidate (CIO 2026-05-05). US-A/B/C/D/E shipped Sprints 19/20/21 + V0.24.x deployed but never run end-to-end in production -- same risk class as Sprint 25 engine-telemetry regression. Sprint 26 closes the gap with a synthetic-but-real drill: stage a known-good release on chi-srv-01 release registry; Pi update-checker daemon detects newer version; Pi runs full self-deploy (download + apply + restart); asserts post-update state. Critical before car-wiring activates trigger every key-on (Spool Pi-wiring concern).

filesToTouch: tests/pi/integration/test_self_update_e2e_production.py (NEW -- spawns real UpdateChecker + UpdateApplier with HTTP transport mocked at urllib edge against staged-but-valid V0.X.Y release; asserts download + apply + restart + .deploy-version updated + connection_log event_type=auto_update_applied row written; would FAIL pre-fix because no test exercises the real production path)
src/pi/update/update_checker.py (UPDATE if gap found in production-path coverage -- audit per US-258 synthetic-test gap; minimal changes preferred)
src/pi/update/update_applier.py (UPDATE if gap found -- same audit)

stories.title: B-047 auto-rollback drill: broken release triggers rollback
stories.intent: Per B-047 D3: Pi watches eclipse-obd.service health post-update; if service fails to reach 'active' within 60 sec, automatically rolls back to previous .deploy-version snapshot. Pi keeps last 3 release snapshots locally. Sprint 26 ships a synthetic drill: stage a deliberately-broken release (e.g., syntax error in main.py); Pi auto-applies; service fails; Pi rolls back. Critical safety net for D8 (no dev/prod separation -- main IS production).

filesToTouch: src/pi/update/update_applier.py (UPDATE if rollback logic missing or broken -- audit existing US-248 code; per B-047 D3 verify watchdog watches systemctl is-active for 60s post-restart; rollback to previous snapshot if not active; preserve happy-path)

tests/pi/integration/test_self_update_rollback_drill.py (NEW -- mocks systemctl is-active to return inactive for 60s after broken-release apply; asserts rollback fires; asserts .deploy-version reverts to prior good version; asserts service is active post-rollback; would FAIL pre-fix if rollback path not wired)

groundingRefs.value: B-047 D3: 60-sec watchdog post-restart; rollback to prior .deploy-version snapshot if service never reaches active; Pi keeps last 3 snapshots
Critical safety net per D8 (main IS production; no staging gate; auto-rollback IS the safety net)

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `rollback|systemctl.*is-active|snapshot.*revert` src/pi/update/. Verify existing US-248 rollback covers D3 60-sec watchdog + 3-snapshot retention.
tests/pi/integration/test_self_update_rollback_drill.py: synthetic broken-release drill; rollback fires; .deploy-version reverts; service active post-rollback; would FAIL pre-fix
Fast suite + new test pass; ruff + sprint_lint clean
invariants: Rollback triggers ONLY on service-not-active within 60s (D3); does NOT trigger on application-level errors that don't crash the service
Rollback preserves the 3-snapshot retention (D3); cleanup oldest-beyond-3 after successful update OR rollback
stopConditions: If existing US-248 doesn't have a 60-sec watchdog at all -- STOP, file as separate gap; this story scope assumes the watchdog exists but is untested

stories.title: B-047 safety-precondition audit: no auto-update during active drive
stories.intent: Per B-047 D2: auto-deploy ONLY when safety preconditions hold: (a) no active drive_id (engine off), (b) sync_log cursor caught up, (c) no in-flight DTC retrieval. Pi-wiring lands this weekend (Spool 2026-05-06) -- update-trigger then fires on every key-on. CRITICAL: must verify the precondition gate actually exists in production code before car-coupled state activates. NEW precondition class added by US-292: DTC-retrieval-in-progress check needs to integrate with the dtc_logger 30s cadence.

filesToTouch: src/pi/update/update_checker.py (UPDATE if gap found -- audit existing US-247 precondition checks; per B-047 D2 verify all 3 conditions checked; if a check missing, add it; minimal changes; integrate US-292 DTC-active check)

tests/pi/update/test_update_checker_safety_preconditions.py (NEW -- 4 parametrized cases: active drive_id present -> skip update; sync_log cursor behind -> skip; DTC retrieval in flight -> skip; all preconditions pass -> proceed; would FAIL pre-fix if any check missing)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/dtc_logger.py post-US-292 (verify how 'DTC retrieval in flight' state is exposed)

stories.title: B-047 cooldown timestamp persistence across reboots
stories.intent: Per B-047 D7: 24-hour cooldown after each completed update-check (regardless of outcome). Failed-check (network error / server unreachable) does NOT enter cooldown. Cooldown survives reboots via persisted timestamp. Sprint 26 audits + tests the persistence. Path: ~/.cache/eclipse-obd/last-update-check.timestamp.

filesToTouch: src/pi/update/update_checker.py (UPDATE if gap -- audit cooldown persistence; ensure last-check timestamp persisted at ~/.cache/eclipse-obd/last-update-check.timestamp; read on boot; respect 24h cooldown across reboots)

filesToRead: src/pi/update/update_checker.py (existing US-247 cooldown logic if any)
doNotTouch: Network-error path (D7 explicitly: failed-check does NOT cool down; preserve)

stories.title: B-053 SyncCadenceController state machine (IDLE/ACTIVE/DRAINING)
stories.intent: Per B-053 Option 2 approved (CIO 2026-05-05): replace constant 5/sec sync polling with engine-aware hybrid. Sprint 26 ships the state machine: IDLE (engine off) = 60s heartbeat; ACTIVE (drive_start fired) = 5s; DRAINING (drive_end fired) = single final flush + return to IDLE. Listens to drive_start / drive_end events from US-200 engine_state. Cadence values mandated upfront per CIO 2026-05-07 'follow Spool's workflow' = post-drive batch review (5s during drive is plenty for Spool's tuning analysis; 1s would be over-engineering).

filesToTouch: src/pi/sync/sync_cadence_controller.py (NEW -- SyncCadenceController class with three states IDLE/ACTIVE/DRAINING; listens to drive_start/drive_end via existing US-200 engine_state event surface; persists last-sync + last-non-empty-sync timestamps; IDLE=60s ACTIVE=5s DRAINING=single flush)

tests/pi/sync/test_sync_cadence_controller.py (NEW -- parametrized state-transition fixture: IDLE->ACTIVE on drive_start; ACTIVE->DRAINING on drive_end; DRAINING->IDLE after flush; missed-drive-start fallback (any non-empty heartbeat auto-switches to ACTIVE); would FAIL pre-fix because controller does not exist)
filesToRead: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py (drive_start + drive_end event-emission surface from US-200)
src/pi/sync/client.py (existing SyncClient that the controller will gate)
doNotTouch: Existing sync trigger wrapper (US-299 wires the controller in)

invariants: Controller does NOT call SyncClient directly (US-299 wires the integration); controller exposes a 'should-sync-now' decision API + state
